---
title: "II. Jesus as God"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
    pdf-engine: xelatex
mainfont: "Arial"
---

# II. Jesus as God


## A. Seed of the Woman
1. Old Testament Prophecy

 a. [Genesis 3:15](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+3%3A15&version=WEB) — "he shall bruise your head, and you shall bruise his heel"

2. New Testament Fulfillments

 a. [Galatians 4:4](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Galatians+4%3A4&version=WEB) — "God sent forth his Son, born of woman…"

 b. [Romans 16:20](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+16%3A20&version=WEB) — "The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet"

 c. [Hebrews 2:14](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+2%3A14&version=WEB) — "…through death he might destroy the one who has the power of death…"

 d. [Revelation 12:9, 17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+12%3A9,+17&version=WEB) — the ancient serpent; war on her offspring


## B. Seed of Abraham
1. Old Testament Prophecy

 a. [Genesis 22:18](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+22%3A18&version=WEB) — "in your offspring shall all the nations of the earth be blessed"

 b. See also [Genesis 12:3](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+12%3A3&version=WEB); 17:19

2. New Testament Fulfillments

 a. [Galatians 3:16](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Galatians+3%3A16&version=WEB) — Paul identifies Jesus as the singular seed

 b. [Galatians 3:29](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Galatians+3%3A29&version=WEB) — believers share the promise through union with Christ

 c. [Matthew 1:1](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+1%3A1&version=WEB) — "the son of David, the son of Abraham"

 d. [Luke 3:34](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+3%3A34&version=WEB) — physical descent from Abraham affirmed


## C. Old Testament Prophecies of a Divine Messiah
1. [Isaiah 9:6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+9%3A6&version=WEB) — "Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace"

2. [Isaiah 7:14](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+7%3A14&version=WEB) — "the virgin shall conceive… Immanuel" (God with us)

3. [Micah 5:2](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Micah+5%3A2&version=WEB) — ruler from Bethlehem "whose origin is from of old, from ancient days"

4. [Jeremiah 23:5–6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Jeremiah+23%3A5-6&version=WEB) — "The Lord [YHWH] is our righteousness"


## D. Melchizedek — Type of Christ's Eternal Priesthood
1. Old Testament

 a. [Genesis 14:18–20](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+14%3A18-20&version=WEB) — king-priest receives tithes from Abraham

 b. [Psalm 110:4](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+110%3A4&version=WEB) — "You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek"

2. New Testament Application (Hebrews)

 a. [Hebrews 7:1–3](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+7%3A1-3&version=WEB) — "having neither beginning of days nor end of life…"

 b. [Hebrews 7:15–17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+7%3A15-17&version=WEB) — Jesus arises in the likeness of Melchizedek

 c. [Hebrews 7:24–25](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+7%3A24-25&version=WEB) — "he holds his priesthood permanently… able to save to the uttermost"


## E. The "Son of Man" — Divine Ruler (Daniel 7)
1. Old Testament

 a. [Daniel 7:13–14](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Daniel+7%3A13-14&version=WEB) — given everlasting dominion over all peoples and nations

2. New Testament — Jesus Claims This Title

 a. [Matthew 26:64](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+26%3A64&version=WEB) — "you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven"


## F. Jesus' Seven "I AM" Statements in John
1. [John 6:35](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+6%3A35&version=WEB) — "I am the bread of life"

2. [John 8:12](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+8%3A12&version=WEB) — "I am the light of the world"

3. [John 10:9](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+10%3A9&version=WEB) — "I am the door"

4. [John 10:11](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+10%3A11&version=WEB) — "I am the good shepherd"

5. [John 11:25](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+11%3A25&version=WEB) — "I am the resurrection and the life"

6. [John 14:6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+14%3A6&version=WEB) — "I am the way, and the truth, and the life"

7. [John 15:1](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+15%3A1&version=WEB) — "I am the true vine"


### Further Study: The Greek egō eimi and Old Testament Background
1. The Greek Phrase

 a. ἐγώ εἰμι (egō eimi) — an emphatic "I am"; Greek normally omits the pronoun since the verb implies it, making its inclusion deliberate

 b. ἐγώ (egō) — Strong's G1473 — "I" or "me"; a primary personal pronoun, used emphatically when expressed

 c. εἰμι (eimi) — Strong's G1510 — "I am," "to be," "to exist"; first-person singular present indicative of the verb "to be"

 d. The phrase can mean: "I am" (identity/existence), "It is I" (reassurance), or in absolute uses — a claim echoing divine self-revelation

2. Connection to the Old Testament

 a. [Exodus 3:14](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+3%3A14&version=WEB) — God reveals his name as אֶהְיֶה אֲשֶׁר אֶהְיֶה (ehyeh asher ehyeh, "I AM WHO I AM"), linked to YHWH — eternal, self-existent being

 b. YHWH derives from the verb hayah ("to be") — essentially "He is" or "He causes to be" — a deliberate echo of the same root

 c. Septuagint (LXX) renders [Exodus 3:14](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+3%3A14&version=WEB) as ἐγώ εἰμι ὁ ὤν (egō eimi ho ōn) — "I am the One Who Is/Existent One" — directly matching John's usage

 d. Isaiah's exclusive divine claims use אֲנִי הוּא (ani hu, "I am He"), rendered in LXX as ἐγώ εἰμι:

  • [Isaiah 43:10–11](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+43%3A10-11&version=WEB) — "I and only I am God… no savior besides me"

  • [Isaiah 43:25](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+43%3A25&version=WEB) — "I am He who blots out your transgressions"

  • [Isaiah 45:18](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+45%3A18&version=WEB) — "I am the LORD, and there is no other"

3. John's Absolute Uses

 a. [John 8:58](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+8%3A58&version=WEB) — "Before Abraham was, I am" — no predicate; pure claim to eternal existence

 b. The audience's reaction (picking up stones, [John 8:59](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+8%3A59&version=WEB)) confirms they understood it as a claim to be God

 c. The "I and only I" force comes from OT context, not the words alone — Jesus embodies YHWH's self-revealed name

4. Echoes in Revelation

 a. [Revelation 1:4](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+1%3A4&version=WEB) — "from him who is and who was and who is to come"

 b. [Revelation 1:8](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+1%3A8&version=WEB) — "says the Lord God, who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty"

 c. [Revelation 4:8](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+4%3A8&version=WEB) — "Holy, holy, holy, is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is to come"


## G. Gospel Accounts — People Declaring Jesus' Deity or Worshiping Him
1. [John 20:28](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+20%3A28&version=WEB) — Thomas: "My Lord and my God!" (clearest verbal affirmation)

2. [Matthew 16:16](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+16%3A16&version=WEB) — Peter: "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God"

3. [Matthew 14:33](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+14%3A33&version=WEB) — Disciples worship after walking on water: "Truly you are the Son of God"

4. [John 1:41](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A41&version=WEB) — Andrew: "We have found the Messiah"

5. [Matthew 2:11](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+2%3A11&version=WEB) — The Magi fall down and worship the infant Jesus

6. [Matthew 28:9](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+28%3A9&version=WEB) — Women at the resurrection take hold of his feet and worship him

7. [Matthew 28:17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+28%3A17&version=WEB) — The eleven disciples worship at the Great Commission

8. [John 9:38](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+9%3A38&version=WEB) — Man born blind: "Lord, I believe." And he worshiped him


## H. Gospel Accounts — People Declaring Jesus as the Christ / Messiah
1. [John 1:41](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A41&version=WEB) — Andrew to Peter: "We have found the Messiah"

2. [Mark 8:29](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+8%3A29&version=WEB) — Peter at Caesarea Philippi: "You are the Christ"

3. [Matthew 16:16](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+16%3A16&version=WEB) — Peter (fuller): "the Christ, the Son of the living God"

4. [Luke 9:20](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+9%3A20&version=WEB) — Peter: "The Christ of God"

5. [John 11:27](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+11%3A27&version=WEB) — Martha: "you are the Christ, the Son of God, who is coming into the world"

6. [John 4:29, 42](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+4%3A29,+42&version=WEB) — Samaritan woman and villagers: "the Savior of the world"

7. [Matthew 14:33](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+14%3A33&version=WEB) — Disciples: "Truly you are the Son of God"

8. [Mark 15:39](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+15%3A39&version=WEB) — Centurion at the cross: "Truly this man was the Son of God!"


## I. Jesus' Pre-Existence Claims (primarily in John)
1. [John 8:58](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+8%3A58&version=WEB) — "before Abraham was, I am"

2. [John 17:5](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+17%3A5&version=WEB) — "the glory that I had with you before the world existed"

3. [John 17:24](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+17%3A24&version=WEB) — "you loved me before the foundation of the world"

4. [John 6:38](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+6%3A38&version=WEB) — "I have come down from heaven"

5. [John 6:62](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+6%3A62&version=WEB) — "the Son of Man ascending to where he was before"

6. [John 16:28](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+16%3A28&version=WEB) — "I came from the Father… going to the Father"

7. [John 8:23](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+8%3A23&version=WEB) — "I am from above… I am not of this world"

8. [John 3:13](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+3%3A13&version=WEB) — "he who descended from heaven, the Son of Man"


## J. Additional New Testament Declarations of Jesus as God
1. [Colossians 1:17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Colossians+1%3A17&version=WEB) — "he is before all things, and in him all things hold together"

2. [Colossians 2:9](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Colossians+2%3A9&version=WEB) — "the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily"

3. [Philippians 2:5–6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Philippians+2%3A5-6&version=WEB) — "in the form of God… equality with God"

4. [Hebrews 1:8](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+1%3A8&version=WEB) — Father speaks of Son: "Your throne, O God, is forever and ever"

5. [Romans 9:5](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+9%3A5&version=WEB) — "the Christ, who is God over all, blessed forever"

6. [Titus 2:13](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Titus+2%3A13&version=WEB) — "our great God and Savior Jesus Christ"

7. [Philippians 2:10–11](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Philippians+2%3A10-11&version=WEB) — "every knee should bow… every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord" ([Isaiah 45:23](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+45%3A23&version=WEB) applied to Jesus)
